event: 39 years old
female
scheduled for carinal resection
adenoid cystic carcinoma in the carina
mass almost totally obstructed the left mainstem bronchus
extended to the carina and right mainstem bronchus
no underlying diseases
preoperative examinations were normal findings
moderate obstructive pattern of the pulmonary function test
forced expiratory volume in one second of 1.88 liter
forced vital capacity of 2.81 liter
their ratio of 67%
general anesthesia was induced
target-controlled infusion of propofol (4 µg/ml)
remifentanil (4 ng/ml)
rocuronium 40 mg
tracheal intubation was performed
right-sided double-lumen tube
moved to the right lateral position
left bronchi and vasculature were dissected
OLV was applied to the dependent lung
arterial oxygen tension (PaO2) was 462 mmHg
right-sided double-lumen tube was replaced with a single-lumen endotracheal tube
bronchial blocker was placed into the RMB
changed to the left lateral position
right thoracotomy was done
peak airway pressure was 28 cmH2O
the PaO2 was 110 mmHg
carinal resection was performed
the LMB was resected
a sterile reinforced endotracheal tube of ID 6.5 mm was inserted into the separated LMB
the airway pressure increased to 35 cmH2O
oxygen saturation by pulse oximetry (SpO2) decreased to 70%
to improve oxygenation by two-lung ventilation
the RMB was resected
an additional sterile endotracheal tube of ID 6.5 mm was also inserted into the separated right bronchus
left and right lungs were ventilated
the SpO2 was restored to 100%
the separated LMB was implanted to neither the trachea nor the right bronchus
the left lung was to be removed
the left endobronchial tube was removed from the surgical field
the non-dependent right lung was only ventilated
the right lung was carefully ventilated with a low tidal volume
the SpO2 decreased below 80%
the tube was immediately reinserted into the LMB
right OLV was reattempted
the SpO2 was gradually decreased below 90%
left pulmonary artery was clamped
right OLV was attempted once again
SpO2 was maintained 100% more than 5 min under right OLV
the left main pulmonary artery was temporary ligated
the left endobronchial tube was removed
the right thoracotomy was closed under right OLV
one hour after ligating the pulmonary artery, the PaO2 was 360 mmHg
left thoracotomy was performed
left pulmonary artery and veins were resected
left pneumonectomy was completed
the patient was shifted to the supine position
tracheal extubation was gently performed
the patient was transferred to the intensive care unit
discharged on the 13th postoperative day
